When people become well-known for things in which they had few advantages, they should deeply recognize that God has been their teacher. Happy are those to whom the Lord gives that greatest victory, conquest, and control over their own spirits. A prayer for further mercy is fittingly begun with thanks for former mercy. There was a special power of God, inclining the people of Israel to be subject to David; it was typical of bringing souls into subjection to the Lord Jesus. People's lives have little substance, considering how many thoughts and cares of an eternal soul are employed about a poor dying body. People’s life is like a shadow that passes away. Even in their greatest earthly honor, believers will remember how unimportant, sinful, and vile they are within themselves; thus they will be protected from self-importance and presumption. God's time to help his people is when they are sinking, and all other helps fail.

New blessings call for new thanksgivings; we must praise God for the mercies we hope for by his promise, as well as those we have received by his providence. To be saved from a harmful sword or wasting sickness without deliverance from the power of sin and the wrath to come is but a small advantage. David requested public prosperity for his people, which is stated. It adds much to the comfort and happiness of parents in this world to see their children likely to do well. To see them as plants, not as weeds, not as thorns; to see them growing, not withered and ruined; to see them likely to bring forth fruit unto God in their day; to see them in their youth growing strong in the Spirit. Plenty is to be desired so that we may be thankful to God, generous to our friends, and charitable to the poor; otherwise, what profit is it to have our storehouses full? Also, uninterrupted peace. War brings many troubles, whether attacking others or defending ourselves. And as we fail to adhere to the worship and service of God, we cease to be a happy people. Those who are subjects of the Savior, the Son of David, share in the blessings of his authority and victories, and are happy because they have the Lord for their God.
